description: Pr[G1.main() @ &m : res] = Pr[G2.main() @ &m : res] -- two games produce the same result distribution

candidates.when: Two games differ in sampling order or have extra inlined procedures
reasoning_hints: Compare left/right programs side by side to find sampling misalignment
swap{side} pos offset moves the statement at <pos> forward by <offset> positions on the given side (1=left, 2=right)
You must read the current goal state to determine the correct N and M -- count statement positions after inline *
wp consumes deterministic assignments from the bottom up
candidates.fallback: Check read/write dependencies between statements. Try swapping on the other side (swap{2}) if swap{1} fails. If statements truly depend, use seq to split the program at a safe point (read goal to determine statement counts). For complex closings after auto, write conseq with explicit postcondition BEFORE auto, not after.

candidates.when: Two games differ by a one-time-pad masking: one adds uniform noise to a message
reasoning_hints: d is uniform, so d + xm is also uniform (bijection on the group)
The two functions are inverses: (d+xm)-xm = d
For XOR-based OTP, use fun h => h +^ msg with addpK as the inverse
Apply wp FIRST to simplify the goal into the shape rnd expects
candidates.fallback: Ensure wp has been applied first. The goal must be in the form: d{1} <$ D ~ d{2} <$ D with a postcondition relating d{1} and d{2}. If not, apply more wp or auto steps first.

candidates.when: Two games are structurally identical or nearly identical
reasoning_hints: sim closes goals where both sides have identical structure
If sim fails, the programs are not syntactically equal -- try inline first
For oracle calls, sim may need call (_: true) or call (_: ={glob A}) first
candidates.fallback: Try: proc; inline *; wp; sim. Or handle oracle calls explicitly with call (_: true); [by sim | ...] before sim on the rest. If you get InvalidGoalShape, specify the postcondition explicitly: byequiv (_: pre ==> ={res}) instead of relying on the default.

candidates.when: Two games differ by flipping a boolean at a specific index (coupling argument)
reasoning_hints: The bijection flips the bit only at position j=i, leaving other bits unchanged
fun b => if j=i then !b else b is its own inverse
This pattern appears in <program> proofs coupling s and s'
candidates.fallback: Check loop invariant is strong enough. The invariant must relate s{1} and s{2} at every iteration, not just at the end.

candidates.when: Proving Pr[Game(NA)] relates to Pr[Game(A)] where NA negates the adversary's output
reasoning_hints: NA (negated adversary) returns !b' instead of b'. So Pr[Game(NA):res] = Pr[Game(A):!res]
The byequiv + conseq + sim pattern: conseq specifies that bit variables match and guess is negated, then sim closes the structural equivalence
After rewriting to Pr[:!res], use Pr[mu_not] to get 1 - Pr[:res], then losslessness
islossless is a one-shot tactic for proving a procedure is lossless
candidates.fallback: The conseq postcondition must name the exact bit and guess variables from the goal. Read the goal state to find them.

description: Pr[G.main() @ &m : res] = 1%r/2%r -- a game returns true with probability exactly 1/2

candidates.when: Game samples a uniform bit b and returns b'=b where b' is independent of b
reasoning_hints: The key insight: b is sampled AFTER the adversary runs, so b is independent of b'
Use swap to move the uniform bit sampling to the very end of the program so rnd (pred1 ...) can consume it
You must read the goal state to find which position the bit sampling is at and compute the swap offset
rnd (pred1 guess_var) says: for any fixed guess, Pr[bit = guess] = 1/2 because bit is uniform
All prior procedure calls must be shown lossless (call oracle_ll for each oracle) before rnd consumes the bit
candidates.fallback: Ensure all adversary calls are consumed (via call + losslessness lemma) before rnd (pred1 b'). The rnd must be the very last sampling.

candidates.when: Proving Pr[game : true] = 1 (losslessness as byphoare goal)
reasoning_hints: rnd predT says the sampling is lossless (always produces a value)
Chain multiple rnd predT for multiple samplings
candidates.fallback: Check that all distributions used are lossless. If a distribution is conditional (dexcepted), you may need mu_bounded lemmas instead.

candidates.when: After Pr[mu_not] rewrite, need to show Pr[:!E] = 1 - Pr[:E]
reasoning_hints: congr structurally matches 1 - Pr[:E] on both sides, leaving Pr[:true] = 1
byphoare + islossless closes Pr[:true] = 1 in one shot
This replaces the verbose pattern: have h1: Pr[:E]+Pr[:!E]=1. rewrite Pr[mu_not]. ring. smt().

WARNING: congr only works when both sides have the shape `1 - Pr[:E]`. If the goal is `Pr[:E] = 1 - Pr[:!E]` (subtraction on the other side, as in <scheme>), congr will fail with 'not a congruence'. In that case, use: have ll : Pr[:true] = 1%r (via byphoare), then rewrite Pr[mu_not] ll /#.
candidates.fallback: If congr fails, the goal shape may not match. For 'not a congruence': the subtraction is on the wrong side — use `have ll : Pr[:true] = 1%r` via byphoare, then `rewrite Pr[mu_not] ll /#` instead.

candidates.when: rnd (pred1 b') fails because postcondition is too complex — weaken it first
reasoning_hints: When rnd (pred1 b') can't close the postcondition directly, weaken with conseq (_: true) => />
Then prove the pred1 obligation manually: move=> b'; rewrite DBool.dbool1E /pred1 => />
islossless handles the remaining losslessness proof as one tactic
For wrapped oracles like Bound(LRO), pass the wrapper explicitly: apply (guess_ll (Bound(LRO))); islossless
candidates.fallback: If islossless fails, the procedure has a non-trivial loop. Fall back to proc_while_variant.

description: |Pr[G1] - Pr[G2]| <= bound, or Pr[G1] - 1/2 <= Pr[G2] -- advantage bounds and security reductions

candidates.when: Final conclusion combining previously proved lemma bounds
reasoning_hints: Negate lemmas with - prefix to flip equation direction
smt() can often close the final arithmetic after rewrites
This is typically the LAST lemma in a security proof file
candidates.fallback: Check lemma names are correct and in scope. Try have h := lemma &m first, then rewrite h.

candidates.when: Game-hopping proof combining multiple intermediate bounds
reasoning_hints: Each have introduces one intermediate result
Inline byphoare proofs (like Pr[Game2]=1/2) can be embedded in have
The final smt() combines all bounds via triangle inequality or transitivity
candidates.fallback: smt() may need explicit lemma hints: smt(h0 h1 h2). If smt times out, try splitting the inequality manually with ler_trans.

description: hoare [M.proc : pre ==> post] or the body of a byphoare after proc

candidates.when: Procedure has a while loop -- need loop invariant and variant
reasoning_hints: The invariant must hold at loop entry (auto closes the base case)
The variant must decrease and be non-negative
For XOR-based invariants: big predT a s +^ big predT a s' = target
sp (simplify program) before while moves assignments into context
candidates.fallback: Strengthen the loop invariant. Common missing pieces: loop counter bounds (0 <= j <= N), index constraints (0 <= i0 < N).

candidates.when: Correctness proof -- procedure is deterministic or simple
reasoning_hints: For cryptographic correctness: expand definitions, then group algebra closes it
log_bij, logg1, logrzM, logDr for group logarithm manipulation
ring works on polynomial equalities, field for division
candidates.fallback: Use have to establish intermediate algebraic equalities, then rewrite them in.

description: islossless M.proc -- procedure terminates with probability 1

candidates.when: Procedure is a sequence of sampling + deterministic steps + calls to lossless oracles
reasoning_hints: Work backward: wp consumes deterministic steps, rnd predT consumes each sampling
For oracle calls, use call Oc_ll where Oc_ll proves the oracle is lossless
Order: call first, then wp, then rnd, then skip
candidates.fallback: Check all distributions are lossless. Conditional distributions (dexcepted) need special handling.

candidates.when: Procedure has a while loop -- need to show termination
reasoning_hints: Variant must be an integer expression that decreases at each iteration and is non-negative (e.g., N - j where j increments)
Read the loop to identify the counter variable and upper bound
The loop body must be shown lossless at each iteration
true as the invariant says we don't care about state -- just termination
candidates.fallback: Ensure the variant is a valid integer expression that decreases. Check loop counter update.

description: Set equality, membership, or restriction properties on fsets/lists

candidates.when: Proving two fsets are equal, or a membership/restriction property
reasoning_hints: fsetP converts set equality to pointwise membership: S1 = S2 <=> forall x, x in S1 <=> x in S2
Unfold /restr, /is_restr to expose the underlying set operations
in_fsetI (intersection), in_fsetU (union), in_fset1 (singleton), mem_oflist, mem_iota for range sets
candidates.fallback: Use have to introduce intermediate facts about membership. For negation, use negP or apply/negP.

description: Pr[M : event = a] = f(a) for all a -- distribution of a specific expression

candidates.when: Proving uniformity: Pr[output = x1] = Pr[output = x2] for all x1, x2
reasoning_hints: bypr reduces equiv to: forall a, Pr[left : event = a] = Pr[right : event = a]
Case split on boolean a (true/false)
For the false case, use Pr [mu_not] to convert Pr[:!E] to 1 - Pr[:E], then losslessness
Need to establish losslessness (Pr[:true] = 1) via byphoare as a have
candidates.fallback: Pr [mu_not] syntax is specific: it rewrites Pr[M : !E] using the mu_not distribution lemma. Make sure the event is in negated form. Use neqF to convert (E = false) to (!E) first.

candidates.when: Computing Pr[M : output = x] = f(x) exactly (e.g., uniform over a set)
reasoning_hints: Split into cases: is x in the support or not?
For x not in support: show probability is 0 via hoare
For x in support: show probability matches the uniform weight
conseq with explicit probability bound transforms the byphoare goal
candidates.fallback: The case split must be exhaustive. Check that the condition covers all possible values of x.

description: IND-CPA security via DDH reduction: embed CPA game inside DDH, extract intermediate game, show Pr=1/2, combine bounds
Prove decrypt(encrypt(m)) = m for an encryption or commitment scheme

phases.goal: hoare or byphoare: Correct(Scheme).main : true ==> res

description: Perfect hiding for a commitment scheme: commitment reveals no information about the message

phases.goal: Pr[Real(A)] = Pr[Ideal(A)] -- real and ideal games are equivalent
Pr[Ideal(A)] = 1/2 -- ideal game is a coin flip

description: Computational binding: extracting a discrete log witness from a binding break

phases.goal: algebraic extraction: from two openings (m,d) and (m',d') with m!=m', extract x = (d-d')/(m'-m)

description: Sigma protocol verification: completeness, special soundness, and SHVZK

phases.goal: completeness: R h w => Pr[Completeness : res] = 1
special_soundness: extract witness from two transcripts

description: Game-hopping security proof: chain of intermediate games with bounds at each hop

phases.goal: For each hop: Pr[G_i] = Pr[G_{i+1}] or |Pr[G_i] - Pr[G_{i+1}]| <= bound_i
Combine all hops: have h0 := hop0 &m; have h1 := hop1 &m; ...; smt()

description: Prove that a program output is uniformly distributed over a set

phases.goal: Pr[M : output = x] = f(x) for all x -- compute the exact distribution
equiv: output distributions are identical for different inputs

description: <program> correctness via lossless + hoare while loop with XOR invariant

IND-CCA security via DDH reduction with game-hopping: embed CCA game inside DDH, chain intermediate games with bad events, bound advantage. Generalizes to any CCA scheme reduced to a hardness assumption via game hops.

phases.goal: DDH1_ex(B(A)) ~ G1 : game with bad event tracking
G1 -> G2 -> ... -> G_final : chain of intermediate games
Pr[G_final : bad] <= qD/order or similar probability bound

description: Rewrite Pr[M @ &m : !E] to Pr[M @ &m : true] - Pr[M @ &m : E]
requires: Losslessness proof to simplify Pr[M : true] = 1

description: Rewrite Pr[M @ &m : E = true] to Pr[M @ &m : E] (remove trivial = true)
syntax: Various approaches: /= or rewrite eqT or congr
requires: Goal has (E = true) or (E = false) inside Pr

description: Reorder program statements in pRHL goals
syntax: swap{side} pos offset -- moves statement at <pos> forward by <offset> positions on <side> (1=left, 2=right). Also: swap{side} [from..to] offset for ranges.
requires: Statements must be independent (no read/write dependency). Read the goal state to count statement positions.
error_fix: Check which variable is read/written. Try swapping on the other side ({2} instead of {1}), or use seq to split the program at a safe point.

description: Discard an extra sampling on one side of a byequiv
requires: One side has a sampling that the other doesn't

description: Split a phoare goal into losslessness + hoare, then combine
syntax: have Hll : islossless M.proc. ... have Hh : hoare [...]. ... by conseq Hll Hh.
requires: A phoare goal that is easier to prove as separate lossless + functional correctness

description: Force a conditional branch in a program to take the true or false path
syntax: rcondt{side} pos -- force the if-then-else at statement <pos> to take the true branch (generates a sub-goal to prove the condition is true). rcondf for the false branch.
requires: A pRHL goal with an if-then-else. Read the goal state to find which statement position the conditional is at.

description: Split a pRHL goal at specific program positions
syntax: seq left_count right_count : (intermediate_condition). -- splits the pRHL proof at a program point, proving the first <left_count> statements on the left match the first <right_count> on the right.
requires: When call or sim can't handle the full program -- split into two parts. Read the goal state to count statements on each side.

description: Cancel multiplication to perform field division in exponent algebra
requires: A goal of the form x * y / y = x or equivalent in a ZModField

description: One-shot tactic to prove islossless goals. Handles proc + inline + rnd predT chain automatically.
requires: Goal must be an islossless judgment. All distributions must be lossless.

description: Close group/ring/field equalities without smt hints
requires: Goal is a polynomial equality in a ring or field (group exponents, ZModField, etc.)

description: Close trivial arithmetic/logic goals with /# (crush)
requires: Goal is a trivial arithmetic or logic obligation (e.g., after byequiv => //)

description: Bound probability of membership: Pr[x in L] <= |L| * max_mu1
syntax: apply (ler_trans ((size l)%r * r)). + by apply mu_mem_le_mu1. apply ler_wpmul2r; smt(mu_bounded). by apply le_fromint.
requires: Goal is a probability bound involving set membership and distribution weight

description: Reference current precondition in seq/while conditions with #pre
requires: Inside a seq or while condition where you want to extend the current precondition

description: When a lemma is exported from a section with a module declaration (e.g., `declare module A : Adv`), applying it outside the section may fail with 'cannot infer module arguments'.
syntax: apply (lemma_name Module). -- pass the module explicitly
requires: Error: 'cannot infer module arguments' when applying a section-exported lemma

description: After `clone Theory as Alias`, lemma names from Theory are renamed under the Alias prefix. The original names are no longer in scope.
requires: Error: 'unknown identifier' when using a lemma from a cloned theory

description: Close group/field exponent equalities in one shot. Handles expressions like g^a^b = g^(a*b), g^a * g^b = g^(a+b), and complex multi-variable group algebra.
requires: Goal is a group/ring/field equality over exponents (ZModField). Works on goals left by `auto => />` in equiv proofs where both sides have group expressions.

description: Normalize group exponent expressions using logarithm bijection and ring arithmetic. Converts nested exponentiation (g^a^b, (g^a * g^b)^c) to flat form (g^(a*b), g^(a*c + b*c)).
syntax: have -> : <nested_group_expr> = <flat_group_expr> by rewrite log_bij !(logg1, logrzM, logDr); ring.
requires: Goal contains group expressions with nested exponentiation that need to be shown equal. Common after `auto => />` in byequiv proofs.

description: Close goals of the form (a = b) = (b = a) using eq_iff.
requires: Goal has a boolean equality with swapped sides, typically after `do !split` in an equiv postcondition.

description: Prove oracle equivalence in byequiv proofs by specifying shared state invariant. Works for any scheme where both sides use the same oracle implementation through different module wrappers.
requires: A byequiv goal where both sides call the same oracle through different module wrappers.
